data.title: Courage Investment Forecasts Profit for 2024
data.description: Courage Investment is expecting to book a profit of no less than $1.6 million for the year ended Dec. 31, 2024, compared with a loss of around $4.0 a year earlier, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Wednesday.

data.title: Metaplanet Now Holds 2,100 Bitcoin, Purchases 68 More BTC
data.description:
Metaplanet (3350) has now accumulated 2,100 bitcoin (BTC), now holding 0.01% of the total BTC supply that will ever be mined, which is 21 million BTC. Metaplanet purchased 68.59 BTC for $6.6 million at an average purchase price of $96,335 per bitcoin.

According to Dylan Le Clair, Metaplanet has raised $20 million in equity capital in the first two trading days of its "21 million plan". Metaplanet also announced on Feb. 18 that they will execute a 10-1 stock split on April 1. This comes just eight months after a reverse a 1-for-10 reverse split.

Metaplanet shares are up over 1% in the current market trading; shares are trading at 6,260 JPY.

data.title: Dubai Taxi's FY24 Profit Slips; Revenue Up
data.description:
Dubai Taxi Co. (DFM:DTC) said Wednesday that its profit for full-year 2024 fell, while revenue from contracts with customers rose year over year.

Profit for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 331.3 million Emirati dirhams, compared with 345.3 million dirhams a year ago. EPS moved to 0.13 dirham from 0.14 dirham.

The Dubai-listed taxi services company's revenue from contracts with customers was 2.20 billion dirhams, compared with 1.95 billion dirhams earlier.

Meanwhile, the board proposed a final dividend of 0.0489 dirham per share for the second half of 2024, payable in April 2025, subject to approval at a general assembly. The final dividend brings the total dividend amount for full-year 2024 to 0.1126 dirham per share.

data.title: Moody’s upgrades credit outlook for LG Electronics to ‘positive’
data.description: Moody’s Investors Service has upgraded LG Electronics’ credit outlook from “stable” to “positive,” the first such revision since 2021. The company’s credit rating remains at Baa2, Moody’s announced on Wednesday. Despite economic uncertainties, including rising tariffs and sluggish demand for certain products, Moody’s said that LG Electronics is expected to maintain financial stability through steady profitability and sound financial indicators. A key factor in the outlook upgrade was the credit improvement of LG Display, a subsidiary in which LG Electronics holds a 36.72 percent stake. Moody’s noted that LG Display’s improving financial position reduces the risk of contingent support from LG Electronics while also enhancing its equity method earnings. Moody’s highlighted LG Electronics’ global brand strength, market leadership, business diversification, and geographic expansion as key credit-supporting factors. It also pointed out that although it was not factored into this rating adjustment, LG Electronics’ ongoing initial public offering (IPO) of its Indian subsidiary could further strengthen its financial standing. LG Electronics filed a preliminary listing application with India’s Securities and Exchange Board at the end of 2024 and aims to sell a 15 percent stake in its Indian subsidiary via the IPO.

data.title: Correction: Auckland International Airport Posts H1 Revenue of NZ$500 Million; EPS of $0.12
data.description:
(Corrects the lede to state that the company reported earnings and the last paragraph to change the currency of the previous dividend to New Zealand dollars)

Auckland International Airport reported Thursday that its earnings per diluted share in the fiscal first half rose to NZ$0.1204, from NZ$0.0798 per diluted share a year earlier.

Analysts polled by FactSet were expecting an EPS of NZ$0.10.

Total income for the six months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was NZ$499.9 million, up from NZ$440.5 million in the same period last year. Analysts surveyed by FactSet expected NZ$494.2 million.

The board declared an interim dividend of NZ$0.074 per share, down from the NZ$0.08 in the previously reported period, payable on April 4 to shareholders on record as of March 20.

data.title: Naver D2SF invests in self-learning autonomous driving startup
data.description: NAVER D2SF, a venture capital unit associated with Naver Corp., has invested in Whereable.ai Inc., a startup in AI-powered autonomous driving technology. Whereable.ai develops an autonomous driving solution based on self-learning AI to recognize and adapt to real-time road conditions. Unlike conventional systems that rely on high-precision maps or cloud processing, its solution uses a plug-and-play approach that allows for immediate deployment without additional infrastructure and enables flexible route and driving area adjustments based on customer needs. Whereable.ai was founded by the original members of ThorDrive, one of South Korea’s pioneering autonomous driving startups. With their extensive experience in commercializing autonomous driving technology in both Korea and the United States, the startup has a wealth of industry expertise.

data.title: Rio Tinto Posts Higher FY24 Attributable Profit; Sales Revenue Down
data.description:
Rio Tinto said Wednesday profit for full year 2024 increased while sales revenue decreased year over year.

Profit after tax attributable to owners for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was $11.55 billion, compared with $10.06 billion earlier. EPS moved to $7.072 from $6.165.

The mining giant's consolidated sales revenue was $53.66 billion, compared with $54.04 billion a year ago.

Meanwhile, the board declared a final dividend of $2.25 per share for the second half, payable April 17 to shareholders on record March 7. It paid $2.58 per share a year ago. This takes the full-year dividend to $4.02 per share, compared with $4.35 per share in the previous year.

data.title: LGES prepares for various scenarios, hinting at Trump’s tariff impacts
data.description: LG Energy Solution, Ltd. (LGES)’s chief executive hinted at potential adjustments to the battery maker’s investment plans in the United States. “We are preparing for various scenarios,” LGES CEO Kim Dong-myung said during a board meeting of the Korea Battery Industry Association on Wednesday. Kim serves as the chairman of the association that represents South Korean battery makers. He added that LGES aims to enhance investment efficiency by optimizing existing facilities rather than making new investments. When asked about Donald Trump’s proposed tariffs on vehicles, Kim refrained from offering detailed remarks, saying that it is an anticipated scenario and that the full impact would become clearer by April 2025. As the largest battery production company in the United States among its competitors, LGES is expected to be one of the main beneficiaries of Trump‘s tariff measures.

data.title: LG Innotek to begin mass production of vehicle AP modules in H2
data.description: LG Innotek Co. is set to begin mass production of its first vehicle application processor (AP) module in the second half of this year, aiming to expand its presence in the growing automotive semiconductor market. According to the company on Wednesday, it is actively promoting its vehicle AP to global semiconductor firms, particularly in North America, ahead of the scheduled production launch. LG Innotek plans to supply the module to semiconductor firms, which will integrate it into final products for global automakers. Acting as the “brain” of next-generation vehicles, the vehicle AP module is a crucial semiconductor component that controls core electronic systems and supports advanced technologies such as autonomous driving and advanced driver assistance systems (ADAS). LG Innotek’s entry into this segment is driven by rising demand for AP modules as autonomous and connected vehicles become more advanced. Traditional printed circuit board (PCB)-based semiconductor chips face limitations in handling the vast data loads generated by high-resolution digital cockpits and sophisticated ADAS features. Industry estimates predict that the global market for vehicle AP modules will grow from 33 million units in 2025 to 113 million units by 2030, expanding at an annual rate of 22 percent. A key advantage of LG Innotek’s AP module is its compact size. Measuring just 6.5 centimeters by 6.5 centimeters, the module integrates more than 400 components, including a system-on-chip (SoC) for data, graphics processing, display, and multimedia control, as well as memory semiconductors and power management integrated circuits (PMICs). The high level of integration allows automakers to reduce mainboard sizes, providing greater design flexibility. The shorter signal transmission distances between components also enhance control performance. LG Innotek plans to further enhance its AP module, improving heat dissipation performance to withstand temperatures of up to 95 degrees Celsius by the end of this year. The company will also use virtual simulations to shorten development cycles. This initiative is part of LG Innotek’s broader strategy to diversify its business portfolio beyond smartphone camera modules, which currently account for 80 percent of its entire revenue. The company is strengthening its position in the automotive sector with products such as automotive cameras, 5G-V2X communication modules, digital keys, and lighting modules. LG Innotek has introduced a 5-megapixel Red-Green-Blue (RGB)-Infrared (IR) camera for vehicles and is actively promoting it to global clients. The camera can be installed in various locations inside a car, such as rearview mirrors and passenger seats, to detect driver drowsiness, monitor attention, and check seat belt usage for passengers in both the front and second-row seats. LG Innotek’s order backlog for automotive components surpassed 13 trillion won ($9 billion) as of the end of last year. The company has also set a target to generate 5 trillion won in annual revenue from its mobility components business by 2030.

data.title: Toyota backs up LG Energy Solution with $1.5 bn order
data.description: Japan’s automaker Toyota Motor Corp. has agreed to shift a $1.5 billion order with LG Energy Solution Inc. to support operations at a Michigan battery plant after General Motors Co. backed out of the deal, according to Bloomberg. As a joint venture between LG Energy Solution and General Motors, the Lansing plant was initially designed to manufacture batteries exclusively for the latter. Since GM announced in 2024 that it would sell its stake in the facility, however, LG Energy Solution has moved to acquire full ownership in the facility. Toyota Motor and LG Energy Solution signed a contract in 2023 to supply 20 gigawatt-hours (GWh) of batteries. As part of the transition, Toyota Motor agreed to shift the 2023 order from another LG plant in Michigan to the Lansing facility, Bloomberg said, citing people familiar with the deal.

data.title: Sika Interplant Systems Gets Board Nod for Stock Split; Shares Up 3%
data.description:
Sika Interplant Systems has received board approval to carry out a stock plit, according to a Wednesday filing to the BSE.

The split will convert each existing equity share with a face value of 10 rupees into five equity shares with a face value of 2 rupees each.

The company has set March 17 as the record date for the stock split.

Shares of the company were up nearly 3% in recent trade.

data.title: Seatrium Secures Drilling Unit Contract From International Maritime Industries
data.description:
Seatrium's offshore jack-up designer, Seatrium Offshore Technology, won an international tender from International Maritime Industries to supply equipment and license for a LeTourneau Super 116E Class Self-Elevating Drilling Unit, Kingdom 3.

The jack-up designs have been customized for operational requirements within the MENA region, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Thursday.

Based on cyber systems, Kingdom 3 will be outfitted with 343 feet of leg and 1.5 million-pound hook load.

Seatrium's shares were down 1% in recent trade.

data.title: Field Solutions Holdings' Creditor Appoints Receivers, Managers
data.description:
Field Solutions Holdings said that Rob Smith and Matthew Hutton, partners of McGrathNicol, were appointed receivers and managers of the company and some of its units, by their secured creditor on Feb 19, according to Wednesday Australian bourse filing.

The move was made following the company's appointment of Barry Wight and Daniel Juratowitch of Cor Cordis as Voluntary Administrators, per the filing.

The receivers now have control of the company which they plan to continue to trade on a business-as-usual basis while pursuing going concern sale options or a recapitalization through the voluntary administration process, the filing said.

The company expects its shares to be suspended until further notice, the filing added.

data.title: Lenovo's Q3 revenue jumps 20% as PC industry recovers
data.description:
Lenovo on Thursday reported a 20% rise in third-quarter revenue as the global personal computer industry showed signs of recovery.

The Chinese technology company, whose main business is making and selling personal computers, reported revenue of $18.8 billion for the quarter ended December 31. That exceeded analysts' expectations of $17.82 billion, LSEG data showed.

Net profit reached $693 million compared with analysts' estimate of $367.7 million.

The global PC market is still facing uncertainty following a post-pandemic slump, with shipments growing by a modest 1.8% during the fourth quarter of 2024, according to research group IDC.

Lenovo maintained its position as the largest PC vendor for the quarter, capturing a 24.5% market share with shipments growing by 4.8%, IDC data showed.

The company has diversified beyond its core PC business in recent years, expanding into software and services. It has also positioned itself to benefit from the booming demand for artificial intelligence (AI) applications by growing its AI server and software businesses.

data.title: Yuan rises after Trump says new trade deal with China is possible
data.description:
Key points:
  • Sentiment improves after Trump says a new China deal is possible
  • Midpoint fixing 1,144 pips firmer than Reuters estimate
  • China leaves LPRs unchanged as expected
  • PBOC's improved macroprudential toolbox key to financial stability -official media

China's yuan strengthened against the dollar on Thursday, as market sentiment improved after U.S. President Donald Trump said a new trade deal with Beijing was possible.

Renewed tariff threats under the Trump administration have been weighing on the yuan in recent months, and the president's latest comment eased investor worries about a further deterioration in the Sino-U.S. trade tensions in the short term, currency traders said.

During Trump's first term as president, a series of tit-for-tat U.S.-China tariff announcements drove the yuan down more than 12% against the dollar between March 2018 and May 2020.

As of 0331 GMT, the onshore yuan was 0.07% higher at 7.2724 to the dollar, while its offshore counterpart traded at 7.2731.

Prior to the market opening, the People's Bank of China (PBOC) set the midpoint rate , around which the yuan is allowed to trade in a 2% band, at 7.1712 per dollar, and 1,144 pips firmer than a Reuters estimate (CNY=RTRS) of 7.2856.

The central bank has set its official guidance on the firmer side of market projections since mid-November, which analysts and traders see as a sign of unease over the yuan's decline.

The yuan's strength also comes as authorities face a delicate balancing act between financial and currency stability and monetary easing, traders and analysts said.

China left lending benchmark loan prime rates (LPRs) unchanged for the fourth straight month in February.

"The U.S.'s relatively mild 10% tariffs on Chinese goods, with room for trade negotiation, suggested that the trade war shocks could be more affordable to China, reducing the urgency for immediate rate cuts," said Ken Cheung, chief Asian FX strategist at Mizuho Bank.

Meanwhile, the state-owned Economic Daily said on Thursday that the central bank's recent improvements to its macroprudential policy toolbox were a key initiative for preventing and fending off financial risks and maintaining the stability of financial markets.

"The global economic and financial situations remain severe and complex, with the adverse impact of changes in the external environment deepening and factors of instability and uncertainty clearly increasing," the newspaper said in an editorial.

The newspaper listed examples of improvements including the central bank's recent move to boost capital flows by allowing companies to borrow more overseas and the regular issuance of yuan bills in Hong Kong to stabilise foreign exchange market expectations and increase market resilience.

Key onshore vs offshore levels:

  • Overnight dollar/yuan swap onshore -8.60 pips vs. offshore -8.60

  • Three-month SHIBOR (SHIBOR=) 1.8 % vs. 3-month CNH HIBOR 2.6 %

data.title: Siemens to Sell 2% Stake in Siemens Healthineers via Private Placement
data.description:
Siemens launched the sale of 22 million shares in Siemens Healthineers , reflecting a 2% stake in the medical technology company.

The shares will be sold through a private placement via an accelerated bookbuild, according to a Wednesday release.

The German technology conglomerate intends to use the net proceeds for general corporate purposes. The company also agreed to a customary lock-up undertaking of 90 calendar days.

data.description: • BLACKSTONE-OWNED AIRTRUNK MULLS ABS BONDS TO FUEL ASIAN GROWTH - BLOOMBERG NEWS

data.title: Lenovo Doubles Profit on Strong Sales
data.description:
(04:02 GMT) *Lenovo Group 3Q Rev $18.80B Vs. $15.72B >0992.HK
(04:03 GMT) *Lenovo Group 3Q Net $693M Vs. Net $337M >0992.HK
(04:03 GMT) *Lenovo Group 3Q Intelligent Devices Group 3Q Rev $13.78B Vs. $12.36B >0992.HK
(04:04 GMT) *Lenovo: Cash Balance Increased 14% on Year >0992.HK
(04:10 GMT) *Lenovo: R&D Expenses Rose 14% to Support AI Innovations >0992.HK
(04:21 GMT) Lenovo Doubles Profit on Strong Sales

By Sherry Qin

Lenovo Group reported better-than-expected quarterly earnings, with device sales strong, boosted by its bet on the fast-growing use of artificial intelligence.

Net profit for the three months ended December more than doubled to $693 million, the world's largest PC maker said Thursday, beating the $381.7 million estimate in a FactSet poll of analysts. The result was partly boosted by a $282 million nonrecurring income tax credit.

Revenue was $18.80 billion, up 20% from a year earlier, also topping analysts' estimates. Lenovo's main business, the intelligent-devices segment, which includes PCs, smartphones and tablets, delivered a 12% revenue gain.

The results reflect a solid commercial PC replacement cycle but also show that the company is reaping the benefits after launching several AI PC models in China last May.

Lenovo Chief Executive Yang Yuanqing told The Wall Street Journal in November that AI PCs could make up 25% of its global PC shipments this year and reach 70%-80% by 2027 as the premium product becomes mainstream.

The company's Hong Kong-listed shares have risen more than 25% so far this year, driven in part by investors' exuberance over Chinese AI startup DeepSeek's breakthrough in January.

Lenovo has been touted as a key beneficiary of the tech advancement. Analysts at UOB said that cheaper, more accessible AI features introduced by DeepSeek could encourage more enterprises, especially small and midsize companies, to invest in AI equipment, which may accelerate replacement demand for PCs and servers.

Write to Sherry Qin at sherry.qin@wsj.com

data.title: Enel Obtains EUR12 Billion Sustainability-linked Revolving Credit Facility
data.description:
Enel (ENEL.MI) secured a sustainability-linked revolving credit facility of 12 billion euros from a syndicate of 26 global financial institutions, according to a Wednesday release.

The new five-year facility replaces the Italian energy company's previous credit line of 13.5 billion euros. It is subject to a spread of 40 basis points above the euro interbank offered rate and comes with a sustainability performance target-based step up/down mechanism that will affect applicable margin.

data.title: Servotech Renewable shares gain 5% on EV charging partnership with France's Watt & Well
data.description:
Shares of Servotech Renewable Power System Ltd rose 5 percent to Rs 127 in morning trade on February 20, extending gains for a second session in a row, after the company signed an agreement with France-based Watt & Well SAS to design, manufacture, and sell EV charger components in India.

Watt & Well specializes in power electronics equipment across aerospace, oil & gas, renewables, and e-mobility sectors.

The collaboration will initially focus on developing a 30kW Power Module for India’s EV charging market, with plans to explore a bidirectional power module for vehicle-to-grid (V2G) applications.

Follow our LIVE blog for all the latest market updates

Under the agreement, Servotech will manufacture these power modules domestically as part of the ‘Make in India’ initiative, while Watt & Well will provide technical expertise. Servotech will also have exclusive rights to market and sell these components in India.

Raman Bhatia, Founder and MD of Servotech, said the partnership would help scale up local EV charger production and reduce dependence on imports. Benoit Schmitt, CEO of Watt & Well, added that the collaboration aims to accelerate EV adoption in India by strengthening domestic supply chains.

Also read: India orders blocking of 119 apps on Google Play Store, majority from China and Hong Kong

Servotech Renewable Power System reported a sharp rise in net profit to nearly Rs 8 crore in the December quarter, compared to Rs 1.11 crore a year ago, driven by higher revenues. Total revenue surged over four times to Rs 216.83 crore from Rs 52.20 crore in the same period last year, while expenses also increased to Rs 204 crore from Rs 50.34 crore. The company, a key player in tech-enabled EV charging and solar solutions, saw strong growth in the quarter.

At about 9:40 am, shares of the company were trading at Rs 123, higher 1.3 percent from the last close on the NSE. Servotech Renewable Power System shares have tanked nearly 30 percent since the start of the year, largely led by the overall bearish sentiment prevailing among investors.

Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by investment experts on Moneycontrol.com are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol.com advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.

data.title: Huadong Medicine's Unit Secures Approval for Filtration Measurement Device
data.description:
Huadong Medicine's unit, Hangzhou Sino-US Huadong Pharmaceutical, has received a medical device registration certificate from China's drug administration for its Transcutaneous Glomerular Filtration Rate Measurement Device.

The newly approved device consists of a host unit, a sensor, and a fixing sticker, and is designed for non-invasive measurement of glomerular filtration rate (GFR). GFR is a key indicator of kidney function.

The company's shares rose more than 1% in recent trade.

data.title: Singtel's Positive FY 2025 Earnings Guidance Signals Confidence — Market Talk
data.description: Singtel's positive earnings guidance for FY 2025 signals confidence, CGS International analysts say in a research report, as they raise the stock's target price to S$3.75 from S$3.70 with an unchanged add rating. Singtel raised its FY 2025 EBIT growth guidance from low double digits to "high teens to low twenties," above the brokerage's expectation of mid-to-high teens. Also, the brokerage's forecasts Singtel's FY 2026 and FY 2027 dividend per share to be 19 and 21 Singapore cents, respectively. These expectations translate into attractive yields of 5.7% and 6.3%, they add. They're also underpinned by Singtel's improving free-cash-flow generation and ongoing execution on monetization initiatives, the analysts add. Shares are unchanged at S$3.37. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Bank of East Asia: Will Seek New Opportunities While Managing Risks Prudently>0023.HK
Most US manufacturers forecast layoffs at Vietnam operations with Trump tariffs, survey shows
data.description:
Key points:
  • AmCham in Vietnam surveyed more than 100 companies
  • Nearly two-thirds of surveyed manufacturers foresee layoffs
  • Vietnam hosts big manufacturing operations of US multinationals

Most U.S. manufacturers surveyed by the American Chamber of Commerce in Vietnam said they would be likely forced to lay off workers if the Trump administration imposed tariffs on the export-reliant Southeast Asian country.

The survey was conducted from February 4-11 over the period when U.S. President Donald Trump had already approved 25% tariffs on steel and aluminium and had pre-announced broader reciprocal tariffs on countries with trade imbalances, and sector-specific duties on semiconductors, cars and pharmaceuticals.

"Among manufacturers, nearly two-thirds foresee potential layoffs," AmCham said, noting the percentage fell to less than half for all businesses.

The survey is based on input from more than 100 of AmCham Vietnam's members, which include big multinational companies such as Intel and Nike .

Vietnam has profited in recent years from big investments by manufacturers that transferred operations from China after Trump imposed tariffs on Beijing in 2018 during his first term.

More than 60% of the country's $500 billion stock of foreign investment is in manufacturing, according to data from Vietnam's government updated to the end of January.

Foreign investors with manufacturing operations in Vietnam had largely remained upbeat after Trump's announcements of tariffs on China, Mexico and Canada at the start of his second presidential term, according to conversations Reuters had with a dozen industry specialists.

But the mood has partly changed with the new tariff threats.

"Everybody was expecting problems but frankly we've been surprised by the so-called reciprocal tariffs as it is a very odd measure," said an investment adviser with a long experience in the Communist-run country. He declined to be named to speak more freely.

Multiple analysts have said Vietnam may become a target of new duties due to its huge trade surplus with the U.S., the fourth biggest among U.S. partners, and may be hit hard by tariffs on semiconductors as it is one of the top chip exporters to the U.S.

Investors in Vietnamese shares accelerated sales in recent weeks.

NEW EXPORT MARKETS

The survey, conducted by the AmCham section in Vietnam's business hubs of Ho Chi Minh City and Danang, found that 81% of respondents expressed concern about potential tariffs, with the percentage growing to 92% among manufacturers.

"Many businesses fear that increased costs due to tariffs could disrupt supply chains and force them to rethink their operations," AmCham said in a press release, noting 94% of manufacturers expected a negative impact.

AmCham said 41% of respondents were considering diversifying their business away from the U.S. market, which is the most important for Vietnam at the moment.

"This shift could see companies redirecting exports to other markets or adjusting supply chains to reduce their reliance on the U.S.," AmCham said.

Companies in other countries are also expressing concern about the impact of U.S. tariffs. Almost nine out of 10 Japanese firms expect Trump's policies to negatively affect business, a Reuters survey showed on Thursday.

data.title: Malaysia Export Growth Below Forecasts
data.description:
Exports from Malaysia grew by 0.3% yoy to MYR 122.8 billion in January 2025, much softer than forecasts of 5%, and sharply easing from a 16.9% jump in December 2024.

It was the fourth straight month of increase but the slowest pace in the sequence.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Global Markets News of the Day - Wednesday at 11 PM ET
data.description:
U.S. Stocks Mixed Amid Tariff Threats

U.S. stocks inched higher, reversing earlier declines, as investors paused to digest Federal Reserve meeting minutes, new housing data and fresh tariff threats from President Trump.

Fed Minutes Reveal Little Appetite for Near-Term Rate Cuts

Central-bank officials cited trade policy as a factor that could hinder inflation progress.

Defense Stocks Wobble as Report Says White House Warned Pentagon of Spending Cuts

Trading on Wednesday illustrates what investors have to look forward to, and why valuation multiples are contracting.

U.S. stocks are back at record highs. Is it too late to chase the bull market?

For the first time in nearly four weeks, the S&P 500 tallied a fresh closing high on Tuesday. Investors who have kept some or all of their portfolios on the sidelines over the past couple of years might be wondering: Is it too late to get in on the action?

U.S. Faces Obstacles on Road to Supplying the World's Natural Gas

There's little doubt the U.S., already the world's top natural gas producer and exporter of LNG, will continue to increase output. But longer term, potential obstacles are more likely to be market related than regulatory.

Bank of Mexico Cuts 2025 Economic Growth Forecast by Half

Central bank now expects growth of 0.6%, down from a previous estimate of 1.2%, given a weak end to 2024 and uncertainty over policies emanating from Washington.

Trump Is Scrambling Global Automakers' Reliance on America

Facing intense competition in China and heavy regulation in Europe, overseas automakers were counting on the U.S. to keep their engines humming. Then came Trump's tariff threats.

U.K. Inflation Reaches 10-Month High

Consumer prices were 3% higher in January than a year earlier, up from 2.5% in December, complicating the picture for the BOE after it said it would cut interest rates gradually.

China Wants Its Economy to Grow 5%. Consumers Aren't Buying It.

Chinese consumers are nervous about the political and economic environments. Their pocketbooks are shut.

BOJ's Takata Calls for Gradual Rate Hikes, Sees Upside Risks to Prices

The Bank of Japan needs to keep adjusting the degree of monetary accommodation in stages, policy board member Hajime Takata said as he warned about inflation risks.
